perturbation.original_text: Mellet shall receive 5,123,878 shares of Sweetskinz common stock (“Shares”) as full consideration for this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Mellet shall receive 5,123,878 shares of Sweetskinz common stock (“Shares”) as initial consideration for this Agreement. Additional consideration may be provided at the sole discretion of Sweetskinz.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the shares are 'initial' consideration, but then allows for 'additional consideration at the sole discretion of Sweetskinz.' This contradicts the original statement that the shares are 'full consideration,' creating uncertainty about the total value Mellet is to receive for the agreement. This contradicts Section 2 where it says 'In consideration of Sweetskinz’s agreement to provide Mellet with the Shares, Mellet agrees:'. Now it is uncertain if the shares are the only consideration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-COMPETITION AND NON-SOLICITATION (a) In consideration of Sweetskinz’s agreement to provide Mellet with the Shares, Mellet agrees: (1) to refrain, directly or indirectly, during the Term from accepting business from, doing business with, inducing or soliciting any Sweetksinz customers to whom Mellet rendered any services during the course of Mellet’s employment with Sweeetskinz, to do business with Mellet, or with any other person or entity, in competition with the type of services performed by Mellet for Sweetskinz except on behalf of and as an authorized representative of Sweetskinz. (2) to refrain, directly or indirectly, during the Term from accepting business from, doing business with or inducing or soliciting, any Sweetskinz vendor who is instrumental in any aspect of the design, development, production or sales of any Sweetskinz product. (3) to refrain, directly or indirectly, during the Term from having any interest in or association with any business which competes with Sweetskinz pneumatic colorized tire products or any other future Products produced by Sweetskinz. Such entities shall include any related entity in which Mellet or his immediate family is a shareholder, director, officer, employee, partner, proprietor, joint venturer, consultant or otherwise, anywhere worldwide. (4) any idea, concept, technique, invention, creation, copyrightable or patentable work ("Product") relating to pneumatic colorized tires or other products sold or developed by Sweetskinz, that Mellet is instrumental in creating, developing or producing, or helping to create, develop or produce, during the time that Mellet is employed by Sweetskinz shall be considered owned by Sweetskinz, whether conceived by Mellet individually or jointly, on or off Sweetskinz's premises. Mellet hereby irrevocably assigns such Product to Sweetskinz and authorizes Sweetskinz to execute on Mellet’s behalf such documents as it may deem necessary to confirm and protect its interests therein. However, Mellet may develop products which do not in any material way compete with any Product of Sweeetskinz so long as such development is of no cost to Sweetskinz, is done after “working hours” (defined as 45-hour work week) and does not interfere with Mellet’s responsibilities as the Chief Technology Officer of Sweetskinz. Mellet acknowledges that this Section 2 is reasonable, among other things, because Sweetskinz anticipates selling its products on all continents of the world and developing relationships with manufacturers, wholesalers and retailers on such continents
perturbation.justification: The original text states the shares are 'full consideration', while the changed text modifies this to 'initial consideration' and allows for 'additional consideration at the sole discretion of Sweetskinz'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that Mellet agrees to certain conditions 'in consideration of Sweetskinz’s agreement to provide Mellet with the Shares'. The changed text makes the 'consideration' unclear, which breaks the original consideration described in the contradicted text. This creates an in-text contradiction, and the perturbed text follows the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: any idea, concept, technique, invention, creation, copyrightable or patentable work ("Product") relating to pneumatic colorized tires or other products sold or developed by Sweetskinz, that Mellet is instrumental in creating, developing or producing, or helping to create, develop or produce, during the time that Mellet is employed by Sweetskinz shall be considered owned by Sweetskinz, whether conceived by Mellet individually or jointly, on or off Sweetskinz's premises.
perturbation.changed_text: any idea, concept, technique, invention, creation, copyrightable or patentable work ("Product") relating to pneumatic colorized tires or other products *currently* sold or developed by Sweetskinz, that Mellet is instrumental in creating, developing or producing, or helping to create, develop or produce, during the time that Mellet is employed by Sweetskinz shall be considered owned by Sweetskinz, whether conceived by Mellet individually or jointly, on or off Sweetskinz's premises. However, Products relating to future products of Sweetskinz are not assigned.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of the word 'currently' limits the scope of the Product ownership to only products currently sold/developed. Then explicitly states future products are NOT assigned. This contradicts the intent of protecting future product development. It contradicts the section before where Mellet cannot be associated with any competitor in future Products. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (3) to refrain, directly or indirectly, during the Term from having any *substantial* interest in or *direct* association with any business which competes with Sweetskinz pneumatic colorized tire products or any other future Products produced by Sweetskinz. Such entities shall include any related entity in which Mellet or his immediate family is a shareholder, director, officer, employee, partner, proprietor, joint venturer, consultant or otherwise, anywhere worldwide. (4) any idea, concept, technique, invention, creation, copyrightable or patentable work ("Product") relating to pneumatic colorized tires or other products *currently* sold or developed by Sweetskinz, that Mellet is instrumental in creating, developing or producing, or helping to create, develop or produce, during the time that Mellet is employed by Sweetskinz shall be considered owned by Sweetskinz, whether conceived by Mellet individually or jointly, on or off Sweetskinz's premises. However, Products relating to future products of Sweetskinz are not assigned.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'currently' to limit the scope of 'Product' ownership to current products and explicitly excludes future products. This directly contradicts the continued validity of clause (3) in the 'Contradicted Text', which restricts Mellet's involvement with competitors concerning 'any other future Products produced by Sweetskinz'. The change creates a clear and direct conflict, making the terms inconsistent and impacting enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees at all times during the Term to hold in the strictest confidence, and not use, except for the benefit of the Parties or to disclose, transfer or reveal, directly or indirectly to any person or entity any confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees at all times during the Term to hold in the strictest confidence, and not use, except for the benefit of the Parties or to disclose, transfer or reveal, directly or indirectly to any person or entity any confidential information, *unless required by law*.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'unless required by law' introduces a significant loophole. While seemingly reasonable, it creates ambiguity about what information is truly protected. This contradicts the original intent of 'strictest confidence'. If confidential information is allowed to be disclosed, this makes other parts of the agreement unclear, mostly related to injunctive relief. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INJUNCTIVE RELIEF: If Sweetskinz so elects, Sweetskinz shall be entitled, in addition to all other remedies available, including but not limited to actual, compensatory and punitive damages, or to specifically enforce the performance by Mellet and to enjoin the violation by Mellet of any provision hereof. Moreover, the parties hereto acknowledge that the damages suffered by Sweetskinz as a result of any violations of Section 2 by Mellet may be difficult to ascertain and Sweetskinz may not have an adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, the parties agree that in the event of a breach by Mellet of Paragraph 2, Sweetskinz shall be entitled to specific enforcement by injunctive relief of Mellet’s obligations to Sweetskinz, without bond and such remedies shall not be deemed to be exclusive of any other remedies available to Sweetskinz, by judicial or arbitral proceedings or otherwise. The remedies referred to above shall not be deemed to be exclusive of any other remedies available to Sweetskinz, by judicial or arbitral proceedings or otherwise, including to enforce the performance or observation of the covenants and agreements contained in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 'strictest confidence' with no exceptions. The changed text introduces 'unless required by law,' creating a loophole that contradicts the Injunctive Relief section. The Injunctive Relief section states that Sweetskinz is entitled to enjoin any violation by Mellet of any provision hereof, and that damages may be difficult to ascertain. The addition to the changed text creates a scenario where Mellet may disclose confidential information if legally required, undermining Sweetskinz's ability to obtain injunctive relief for breaches of confidentiality as envisioned in the contradicted text, and creating an inconsistency as described in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Moreover, the parties hereto acknowledge that the damages suffered by Sweetskinz as a result of any violations of Section 2 by Mellet may be difficult to ascertain and Sweetskinz may not have an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.changed_text: Moreover, the parties hereto acknowledge that the damages suffered by Sweetskinz as a result of any violations of Section 2 by Mellet may be difficult to ascertain and Sweetskinz may not have an adequate remedy at law. *However, in no case shall damages exceed $10,000.*
perturbation.explanation: The addition of a damages cap introduces a contradiction. The original clause acknowledges damages may be difficult to ascertain and Sweetskinz may not have an adequate remedy at law, suggesting potentially large damages. The cap limits the potential recovery. This introduces a contradiction in how damages can be enforced, making the acknowledgement of 'no adequate remedy at law' false. This in-text contradiction contradicts Section 4 where there is injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INJUNCTIVE RELIEF: If Sweetskinz so elects, Sweetskinz shall be entitled, in addition to all other remedies available, including but not limited to actual, compensatory and punitive damages, or to specifically enforce the performance by Mellet and to enjoin the violation by Mellet of any provision hereof. Moreover, the parties hereto acknowledge that the damages suffered by Sweetskinz as a result of any violations of Section 2 by Mellet may be difficult to ascertain and Sweetskinz may not have an adequate remedy at law. *However, in no case shall damages exceed $10,000.* Accordingly, the parties agree that in the event of a breach by Mellet of Paragraph 2, Sweetskinz shall be entitled to specific enforcement by injunctive relief of Mellet’s obligations to Sweetskinz, without bond and such remedies shall not be deemed to be exclusive of any other remedies available to Sweetskinz, by judicial or arbitral proceedings or otherwise. The remedies referred to above shall not be deemed to be exclusive of any other remedies available to Sweetskinz, by judicial or arbitral proceedings or otherwise, including to enforce the performance or observation of the covenants and agreements contained in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a cap on damages of $10,000. The contradicted text includes the same change. The original contradicted text states that damages may be difficult to ascertain and that Sweetskinz may not have an adequate remedy at law. This suggests the possibility of damages exceeding $10,000. The addition of the cap directly contradicts the assertion that there may not be an adequate remedy at law. The cap limits potential recovery and creates an inconsistency in how damages can be enforced, making the acknowledgement of 'no adequate remedy at law' false.

perturbation.original_text: It is agreed that if any legal action or other proceeding is brought for the enforcement of this Agreement, or because of an alleged dispute, breach, default or misrepresentation in connection with any provision of this Agreement the successful Party or Parties, as the case may be, shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorney’s fees and other costs incurred in that proceeding in addition to any other relief or judgment to which it may be entitled.
perturbation.changed_text: It is agreed that if any legal action or other proceeding is brought for the enforcement of this Agreement, or because of an alleged dispute, breach, default or misrepresentation in connection with any provision of this Agreement the successful Party or Parties, as the case may be, shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorney’s fees and other costs incurred in that proceeding in addition to any other relief or judgment to which it may be entitled. *However, attorney's fees shall not be awarded if the damages recovered are less than $5,000.*
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by creating a condition where attorney's fees are not awarded if the damages recovered are low. This conflicts with the original clause, which states that the successful party 'shall' be entitled to recover attorney's fees, without exception. This affects the ability to enforce the whole agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ATTORNEY’S FEES: It is agreed that if any legal action or other proceeding is brought for the enforcement of this Agreement, or because of an alleged dispute, breach, default or misrepresentation in connection with any provision of this Agreement the successful Party or Parties, as the case may be, shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorney’s fees and other costs incurred in that proceeding in addition to any other relief or judgment to which it may be entitled. *However, attorney's fees shall not be awarded if the damages recovered are less than $5,000.*
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a caveat ('However, attorney's fees shall not be awarded if the damages recovered are less than $5,000.') to the original clause, which states the successful party 'shall' be entitled to recover attorney's fees without exception. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text and shows that the exception to awarding attorney's fees when damages recovered are less than $5,000.00 has been added in two different locations. This contradicts the original intent by creating an exception where there was none and contradicts the obligation within the contract itself, thus an in-text contradiction.
